title: When you change the storage driver we ignore the storage-options
body:
The storage driver and the storage options in storage.conf should
match, but if you change the storage driver via the command line
then we need to nil out the default storage options from storage.conf.

If the user wants to change the storage driver and use storage options,
they need to specify them on the command line.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Check with @mheon; there was some discussion earlier today about using per-driver opts using sections, e.g. `[storage.options.vfs]`, `[storage.options.overlay]`, etc; but it needs coordination with containers/storage.

I would rather not complicate those matters,  It would become a ADMIN nightmare, unless we support multiple drivers simultaneously.   I think this fixes the issue until we decide to support multi drivers.

@mheon @rhatdan With this applied, I'm still seeing [failures in F30 when running rootless](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5609254338166784). The error message is the same as before this PR, bit I'm not sure if cause is the same:  `vfs driver does not support mount options`.

If you look one line up from the error you'll see
```
[+0381s] Running: .../podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir [...] --storage-driver vfs
```
From a quick look this appears to be `test/utils/utils.go:77`, last touched by f610a485c by @baude. 

Does anyone know when exactly the vfs driver started griping about options? It sounds like that's new.

Good catch Ed.

@baude perhaps when running rootless, those tests need to set `nocache=true`?  Though a simpler option may be to just not use the image cache when running rootless (I'm not sure what the de-optimization penalty is).
Related, I also get [failures on normal, root-run F30 tests](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5974003529809920).  Though perhaps worse, some give no error message relates to the storage driver :confused:

@mheon @baude @cevich @edsantiago @TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe @vrothberg I don't see why this should not be merged?  
Can I get some LGTM. Or do we still need more changes?
@rhatdan it seems to not  fix problem in Fedora 30...or we're interpreting the test results incorrectly.  I'll rebase #3078 against this PR and run again just to be double-sure...

@rhatdan I think there's something else going on that I can't figure out. Basically, no matter what I do, I cannot reproduce the failure by using the `--storage-opt` command-line option:
```
# podman --storage-driver=vfs info
[all ok]
```
It's only when I hand-edit `/etc/containers/storage.conf`, change driver to "vfs", but leave `mountopt=` untouched, that's when I get the "vfs driver does not support mount options" error.

I would prefer to have a solid understanding of the problem before we merge this. (I'm also concerned about the approach you take here, because I'm not sure if it would clobber command-line `--storage-opts`)

More weirdness:
```
# grep ^driver /etc/containers/storage.conf
driver = "vfs"                   ! hand-edited
# rm -rf /var/lib/containers;podman --storage-driver=overlay info
Error: could not get runtime: overlay: Unknown option vfs.mountopt
```
I obviously do not understand the `--storage-driver` option.

Continuing weirdness. This is not behaving as I expect it. I think this needs a good regression test suite.
```
# rm -rf /tmp/ttt;podman --storage-driver=vfs --storage-opt=dsf --root=/tmp/ttt info|grep -1 Graph
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - dsf          <------ I would not expect to see options when vfs is used
  GraphRoot: /tmp/ttt
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
```
/hold
do not merge this yet
So my patch should fix any use case other then when you make the storage.conf file inconsistent.

@rhatdan thanks, I now understand the `--storage-opt` option better; but I still don't see what this patch accomplishes. `--storage-driver=vfs` does not seem to generate errors no matter what I try; only if I hand-edit storage.conf
im hoping to get root cause here, i have frozen the ci vm that does this testing so i can poke around when it finishes.
also, @rhatdan this appears to throw away --storage-opts provided by the user on the command line

@baude works for me

```
 ./bin/podman --storage-opt=abcd --storage-driver=vfs --storage-opt=1234 --root=/tmp/ttt info 
...
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - abcd
  - "1234"
  GraphRoot: /tmp/ttt
  GraphStatus: {}
```
@rhatdan now try that *without* your patch...

@edsantiago Without this patch we get
```
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on
```
Which does not cause an issue, but is still wrong according to the documention and could cause issues in the future.

Without my patch specifying the graphdriver-options seems to override the storage.conf, which I am not sure is expected.

```
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - abcd
  - "1234"
```

This behaviour matches the documentation.

```       --storage-opt=value

       Storage driver option, Default storage driver options are configured in
       /etc/containers/storage.conf ($HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf in
       rootless mode). The STORAGE_OPTS environment variable overrides the
       default. The --storage-opt specified options overrides all.
```
So I would say this is correct.
@edsantiago Please test this out, to see if it fixes your --storage-driver=vfs --privileged problem?

Addresses the problem as described, with reproducer, in #3263.

One behavior surprises me:
```
# ./bin/podman --conmon $CONMON_BINARY --root=/tmp/ttt --storage-driver=vfs --storage-opt=vfs.metacopy=on run --privileged alpine date
```
With the command-line `--storage-opt` override I was expecting to see the `does not support mount options` error; but the above works perfectly fine. LGTM either way, but I find this odd.

@edsantiago we document that setting the --storage-driver overrules the storage-options set in the storage.conf file. So it ignores those settings, which is why it does not give any errors.

@edsantiago @mheon @baude @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat I believe this is ready to merge.
LGTM
/lgtm
All green, @mheon should we cancel @baude's hold on this?
/hold cancel
